topic: steep well-oriented slopes, solar interception, and ripening
trigger_keywords: afirmaciones sobre los viñedos del Mosel
pendientes pronunciadas del Mosel favorecen la maduración del Riesling

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Vines grow on a steep slope whose aspect is favourably oriented toward the sun in a cool region.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The slope angle and aspect improve interception of direct sunlight, raising canopy and bunch temperatures and supporting photosynthesis in otherwise marginal ripening conditions.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Grapes can ripen more reliably and develop greater flavour and sugar maturity than on a poorly exposed site; steepness alone does not guarantee this effect.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: A steep, well-oriented slope can improve solar interception and support ripening in a cool region, but aspect and local conditions remain essential.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
